conjecture_title: Research direction: minimal heroic tournament families
paper_title: Extension of Gyarfas-Sumner conjecture to digraphs
summary: No paper has been found that directly addresses the characterization of all finite minimal families of tournaments forming heroic sets of the form $\{\overrightarrow{K_2}, \overleftrightarrow{K_2}, H_1, \ldots, H_k\}$; the single-hero case ($|\mathcal{H}|=1$) was already settled by Berger et al. before the source paper. Related activity around heroic sets and dichromatic number bounds has continued (heroes in oriented complete multipartite graphs, chi-boundedness for specific forbidden structures), but the general multi-tournament minimal family characterization remains open. Confidence is medium rather than high because the paper is over five years old and related heroic-set questions have attracted ongoing attention.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper exists (Decomposing and colouring some locally semicomplete digraphs, Aboulker–Aubian–Charbit, 2021/2022). WebFetch confirms it does not discuss heroic sets or the minimal heroic tournament families direction; it proves a conjecture about oriented graphs whose out-neighborhoods are transitive tournaments (dichromatic number ≤ 2). The internal reference contribution ($F=S_2^+$, $H=C_3$) relates to a different conjecture from arXiv:2009.13319 about chi-boundedness, not this research direction.

Paper exists ((P6, triangle)-free digraphs have bounded dichromatic number, Aboulker–Aubian–Charbit–Thomassé, 2022/2023). WebFetch confirms it does not discuss heroic sets or the minimal heroic tournament families direction; it bounds the dichromatic number for (P6, triangle)-free digraphs. The internal reference contribution ($\overrightarrow{P}_6$-free, $\omega(D)\leq 2$) relates to a different conjecture from arXiv:2009.13319, not this research direction.

search_queries: heroic set tournament digraph minimal Aboulker Charbit Naserasr
notes: Both internal references address other conjectures from arXiv:2009.13319 (chi-boundedness and dichromatic number for specific forbidden structures) rather than the minimal heroic tournament families research direction. A related verified paper, arXiv:2202.13306 (Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit 2022, 'Heroes in oriented complete multipartite graphs'), characterizes heroes in oriented complete multipartite graphs and quasi-transitive oriented graphs, showing heroes there coincide with those in tournaments (with one unresolved 6-vertex exception); this is related background but concerns individual-tournament heroic sets (closer to the already-resolved $|\mathcal{H}|=1$ case), not the multi-tournament minimal family characterization. No follow-up paper specifically addressing the multi-tournament minimal heroic family characterization was found within the 5-call budget.
